_comment: Golden inputs for the scoring pass (app/evals/live_scoring.py). Each case is a hand-authored SYNTHETIC applicant + one dimension, run through the REAL production scoring prompt+model and graded deterministically: the produced score must fall in the expected [score_min, score_max] band (+ optional confidence). Fictional data only (no real applicants), so it is safe to commit. Uniform envelope (docs/eval-case-schema.md): `metadata` is harness-only (no model, live or judge, ever sees it); `given` is what the scoring prompt receives. No judge/produced block — the Judge tab audits these same cases blind (it re-produces a score from `given` and the harness compares to `metadata.expected`). Grow by adding cases; keep applicants terse.
judge_background: This pass scores ONE applicant against ONE ranking dimension on a signed −1..+1 scale. +1 means the applicant strongly exhibits the dimension's high_end; −1 means they demonstrably exhibit the low_end; 0 (neutral) means the applicant is SILENT on the dimension — absence of evidence is neutral, never negative. Confidence reflects how much explicit evidence the applicant gave and if they gave no evidence, this is low confidence. Score only what the cited evidence supports; do not infer unstated facts.

metadata.note: Applicant never mentions trade skills -> silence must score 0 (neutral), never negative.

essays.introduce_yourself: We love reading and community potlucks.
essays.why_coop: We want neighbours who look out for each other.

dimension.name: Licensed trade skills
dimension.definition: Whether an adult holds a licensed/certified trade (electrician, plumber, gasfitter, Red Seal) they explicitly offer toward co-op maintenance.
dimension.high_end: At least one adult holds a current trade licence and explicitly offers it for co-op maintenance.
dimension.low_end: No adult holds or mentions any licensed trade.

essays.skills: I'm a Red Seal electrician and I'll handle the co-op's electrical maintenance and safety checks myself.
essays.why_coop: I want to contribute my trade to a place I live.

dimension.name: Licensed trade skills
dimension.definition: Whether an adult holds a licensed/certified trade (electrician, plumber, gasfitter, Red Seal) they explicitly offer toward co-op maintenance.
dimension.high_end: At least one adult holds a current trade licence and explicitly offers it for co-op maintenance.
dimension.low_end: No adult holds or mentions any licensed trade.

metadata.note: Applicant addresses the dimension and states a genuine limitation -> a real negative, not silence.

essays.available_time: I'm honest that between touring most weekends and two other boards I sit on, my calendar is packed — I could only manage the bare minimum co-op hours.

dimension.name: Realistic available time for co-op work
dimension.definition: How much discretionary time the household realistically has for co-op shifts and committee work after accounting for self-reported external commitments.
dimension.high_end: Sparse outside commitments and abundant free time; offers more than the minimum share.
dimension.low_end: A genuinely packed schedule that leaves only minimum hours for co-op duties.

metadata.note: The regression that motivated the signed scale: low_end is WORDED as absence, applicant is silent -> must still be 0, not negative.

essays.introduce_yourself: We're quiet, reliable, and keep to ourselves mostly.

dimension.name: Governance / board experience
dimension.definition: Prior experience serving on a board, council, or committee that involved shared decision-making.
dimension.high_end: Has served on a board/council/committee and describes the governance work.
dimension.low_end: No board, council, or committee experience mentioned anywhere.

metadata.note: Real but thin evidence -> a moderate positive mid-band, not rounded up to the high pole. Guards the middle of the scale (the existing cases only pin the poles and neutral).

essays.skills: I helped organise the tool-share at our last building and can pitch in on small repairs — I'm handy but not licensed.
essays.why_coop: We like fixing things ourselves and sharing what we have.

dimension.name: Hands-on maintenance willingness
dimension.definition: Whether the household is willing and able to pitch in on general upkeep and small repairs — no licence required, just demonstrated handiness and a stated willingness to help.
dimension.high_end: Describes substantial hands-on maintenance experience and an explicit commitment to lead or carry repair work for the co-op.
dimension.low_end: States no interest or ability in hands-on upkeep.
